prompt_replacements.old: If the question asks for counts per group, by group, or for each specified key, use grouped_aggregate.
prompt_replacements.new: If the question asks for any aggregate measure once per group, including a count, sum, average, minimum, or maximum, use grouped_aggregate.

prompt_replacements.old:
answer_expression.group_key.description names the result key or grouping dimension, such as region, period, category, or supplied key.
Use answer_expression.group_key.domain=SPECIFIED_QUESTION_INPUTS when groups are exactly declared question_inputs; otherwise use SOURCE_RESULT_VALUES.
For a repeated measure over specified inputs, set answer_expression.group_key with domain=SPECIFIED_QUESTION_INPUTS and put one measure/count result column in answer_outputs, not one output per key value.
prompt_replacements.new:
answer_expression.group_key.description names the result key or grouping dimension.
Use value_source.kind=source_value when an existing source value is the group key.
Use value_source.kind=temporal_bucket with grain when timestamps are divided into calendar buckets.
Use value_source.kind=specified_question_inputs when concrete supplied inputs are the group members.
For a repeated measure over specified inputs, put one measure/count result column in answer_outputs, not one output per key value.

prompt_replacements.old:
Use grouping_grain when a supplied unit such as day, week, month, or quarter states how source values are divided into result groups.
A grouping_grain is neither the queried time period nor a group member. For 'events per day during March,' day is the grouping_grain and March is the time_value.
prompt_replacements.new: A temporal grouping grain configures a temporal_bucket group key; it is not a question input.

prompt_replacements.old: Before finalizing question_inputs, actively inventory every word or phrase that is a reference value, predicate value, time value, threshold value, formula value, grouping grain, result limit, or resolved row-set reference.
prompt_replacements.new: Before finalizing question_inputs, actively inventory every word or phrase that is a reference value, predicate value, time value, threshold value, formula value, result limit, or resolved row-set reference.

prompt_replacements.old: Each question_input must have one primary contract role: population predicate operand, result key, time constraint, grouping-grain operand, computation operand, or result limit.
prompt_replacements.new: Each question_input must have one primary contract role: population predicate operand, specified group member, time constraint, computation operand, or result limit.

prompt_replacements.old: Create question_inputs for concrete values supplied by the question or conversation resolution that a population predicate, result key, time constraint, compute expression, or result limit consumes.
prompt_replacements.new: Create question_inputs for concrete values supplied by the question or conversation resolution that a population predicate, specified group-member set, time constraint, compute expression, or result limit consumes.

prompt_replacements.old: Then list every reference_value, predicate_value, time_value, threshold_value, formula_value, grouping_grain, and result_limit the question contains and outcome must declare without assigning owners or predicates.
prompt_replacements.new: Then list every reference_value, predicate_value, time_value, threshold_value, formula_value, and result_limit the question contains and outcome must declare without assigning owners or predicates.

prompt_replacements.old:
Within each answer_request, author answer_expression, then answer_population, then question_input_uses.
question_input_uses assigns each input used by this answer_request to exactly one semantic owner kind.
Create exactly one question_input_uses record for each fact-local input_ref.
Use GROUP_KEY when the input defines a requested result group under SPECIFIED_QUESTION_INPUTS.
A SPECIFIED_QUESTION_INPUTS group key restricts results to its GROUP_KEY inputs and groups by them.
GROUP_KEY inputs are not candidate-row predicates; create no EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT for them.
Use POPULATION_TESTS when the input is an operand of one or more EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT membership tests.
Use COMPUTE_EXPRESSION when a formula_value is an operand in the requested computed_scalar expression.
Use GROUP_KEY_DERIVATION when a grouping_grain determines how source values are converted into a SOURCE_RESULT_VALUES group key.
Use RESULT_LIMIT when the input supplies answer_expression's requested result limit.
Each EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT lists the input_ref of every POPULATION_TESTS operand it consumes in question_input_refs.
One population test may reference several POPULATION_TESTS inputs, and one POPULATION_TESTS input may be referenced by several tests.
question_input_refs may name only POPULATION_TESTS inputs. GROUP_KEY, GROUP_KEY_DERIVATION, COMPUTE_EXPRESSION, and RESULT_LIMIT inputs are never membership-test operands.
SUBJECT_IDENTITY asks only whether the candidate is an instance of answer_subject and has no input operands.
An input identifying a related entity uses POPULATION_TESTS unless it is a SPECIFIED_QUESTION_INPUTS group member, in which case it uses GROUP_KEY only.
SUBJECT_IDENTITY, NORMAL_INSTANCE_GUARD, and RAW_RECORD_GUARD use an empty question_input_refs array.
prompt_replacements.new:
Within each answer_request, author answer_expression, then answer_population, then question_input_uses.
question_input_uses assigns each input used by this answer_request to exactly one semantic owner kind.
Create exactly one question_input_uses record for each fact-local input_ref.
Use GROUP_KEY exactly for concrete members of a specified_question_inputs group key; source_value and temporal_bucket group keys have no GROUP_KEY inputs.
A specified_question_inputs group key restricts the result-key domain to its GROUP_KEY inputs and groups by them; do not also create population predicates for those inputs.
Use POPULATION_TESTS when the input is an operand of one or more EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT membership tests.
Use COMPUTE_EXPRESSION when a formula_value is an operand in the requested computed_scalar expression.
Use RESULT_LIMIT when the input supplies answer_expression's requested result limit.
Each EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT lists every POPULATION_TESTS input_ref it consumes in question_input_refs.
One population test may reference several POPULATION_TESTS inputs, and one POPULATION_TESTS input may be referenced by several tests.
GROUP_KEY, COMPUTE_EXPRESSION, and RESULT_LIMIT inputs are never membership-test operands.
SUBJECT_IDENTITY, NORMAL_INSTANCE_GUARD, and RAW_RECORD_GUARD use an empty question_input_refs array.

prompt_replacements.old: Before returning a complete contract, verify that each copied question occurrence is declared once, every fact-local input appears once in question_input_uses, group-key and compute-expression inputs appear in no population test, and every POPULATION_TESTS input is referenced by at least one EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT.
prompt_replacements.new: Before returning a complete contract, verify that each copied question occurrence is declared once, every fact-local input appears once in question_input_uses, group-key and compute-expression inputs appear in no population test, and every POPULATION_TESTS input is referenced by at least one EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT.
